signals.idea: Make the video editor switch to condensed mode when the window is too short to show the video preview at proper dimensions. Currently MIN_STANDARD_HEIGHT is a hardcoded sum of fixed pixel values (line 133 of VideoEditorShell.tsx) that ignores the video aspect ratio. Fix: compute the minimum preview height from the video aspect ratio and available width. The video dimensions are in editorData.resolvedConfig.output.resolution (e.g. '1920x1080'), parsed via parseResolution() from config-utils.ts. The preview panel fills the available width (minus properties panel 360px, padding, etc), so: minPreviewHeight = availablePreviewWidth / aspectRatio. The threshold should be: minPreviewHeight + MIN_TIMELINE_HEIGHT + chrome overhead. Use the ResizeObserver entry.contentRect.width to get available width, compute the aspect-ratio-aware minimum, and compare against entry.contentRect.height. This way: (1) a 16:9 video needs less height than a 9:16 portrait video, (2) narrower windows need less preview height so the threshold adapts, (3) the transition still happens naturally via ResizeObserver. Key file: VideoEditorShell.tsx lines 132-148. The editorData.resolvedConfig is already available in FullEditorLayout.

unresolved_flags.concern: Video-editor shell: the plan’s width/aspect-based condensed threshold ignores the existing standard-layout maximize-timeline path, which already fixes the preview row at 180px. In this repo that means portrait timelines could be forced into condensed mode on normal desktop heights, effectively taking away the non-condensed maximize-timeline workflow unless the plan explicitly scopes the new threshold around `isTimelineMaximized` or otherwise accepts that behavior change.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 2.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 1.

items.criterion: Condensed mode threshold accounts for video aspect ratio: portrait videos (e.g. 9:16) trigger condensed at a higher window height than landscape (16:9)
When resolvedConfig is not yet loaded, falls back to 16:9 aspect ratio (no crash, no NaN)
MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT (180px) remains a floor — threshold never drops below the original minimum for very wide windows
ResizeObserver still fires on width changes (not just height), so narrowing the window also updates the threshold dynamically
No flickering/oscillation at the condensed mode boundary during normal resize
PROPERTIES_AND_GAPS constant matches actual layout spacing from the grid template
Change is contained to VideoEditorShell.tsx (single file, ~15 lines)

unresolved_flags.concern: Video-editor shell: the plan’s width/aspect-based condensed threshold ignores the existing standard-layout maximize-timeline path, which already fixes the preview row at 180px. In this repo that means portrait timelines could be forced into condensed mode on normal desktop heights, effectively taking away the non-condensed maximize-timeline workflow unless the plan explicitly scopes the new threshold around `isTimelineMaximized` or otherwise accepts that behavior change.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current condensed switch is a simple height check at `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:133-146`, while the standard layout still allows `isTimelineMaximized ? `${MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT}px auto 1fr`` at `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:150-152`. The standard grid keeps the wide preview column in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:428-436`, and the preview component itself renders inside a fixed-height container (`min-h-[220px]`) at `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx:92-95`.
